error.signature: nginx: [emerg] "server" directive is not allowed here

verdict.summary: nginx config syntax error. Directive placed in wrong context (e.g., server block inside another server block).

dead_ends.action: Copy config snippets from Stack Overflow without understanding context hierarchy
dead_ends.why_fails: nginx has strict context nesting: main > http > server > location. Directives in wrong context fail.

dead_ends.action: Put all configuration in a single nginx.conf file
dead_ends.why_fails: While valid, large monolithic configs are error-prone. Use include and sites-enabled/ pattern.

workarounds.how: sudo nginx -t  # validates config and shows exact error location
http {} contains server {}; server {} contains location {}; main context is outside http {}

workarounds.action: Use include to split config into manageable files
